title: Minimize the admission of containers with added capabilities

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers with added capabilities
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers with capabilities assigned beyond the default set.
input_row.rationale: Containers run with a default set of capabilities as assigned by the Container Runtime. Capabilities outside this set can be added to containers which could expose them to risks of container breakout attacks. There should be at least one PodSecurityPolicy (PSP) defined which prevents containers with capabilities beyond the default set from launching. If you need to run containers with additional capabilities, this should be defined in a separate PSP and you should carefully check RBAC controls to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to access that PSP.
input_row.audit: Get the set of PSPs with the following command: kubectl get psp Verify that there are no PSPs present which have allowedCapabilities set to anything other than an empty array.
input_row.remediation: Ensure that allowedCapabilities is not present in PSPs for the cluster unless it is set to an empty array.
input_row.impact: Pods with containers which require capabilities with the default set will not be permitted.
input_row.default_value: By default, PodSecurityPolicies are not defined.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions using `kubectl get psp`, which indicates that the property can be queried via Kubernetes APIs or CLI commands.
- The control is focused on PodSecurityPolicies (PSPs) and their configuration, which is a standard Kubernetes resource.
- The rationale discusses the technical implications of allowing additional capabilities, aligning with security best practices that can be evaluated programmatically.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_allowed_capabilities_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl get psp -o json` to retrieve all PodSecurityPolicies in the cluster.
- Check the `allowedCapabilities` field in each PodSecurityPolicy object.
- Pass criteria: Ensure that `allowedCapabilities` is either not present or set to an empty array (`[]`).
- If any PSP has `allowedCapabilities` set to a non-empty array, it indicates a compliance failure.
